entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: Learn and practice lettering techniques to create meaningful and inspirational hand-lettered art. Lesson topics include serifed, sans-serifed, and script fonts, line width and value, guidelines, outlines, and shadow lines, key principles of design, dimension, and shading. Each term concludes with a project, and skills can be used to create beautiful collections of mottoes and quotes in a Commonplace Book or for display or gifts.

rows.label: Lettering: Level 1
rows.books: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Lettering: Level 1
☐ Allow time for loading and viewing the tutorial video each time you begin a new style. Then, plan your lesson time accordingly.

☐ Purchase supplies, then organize and store them where they can be easily accessed.

☐ If you haven't done so already for Art Instruction lessons, prep your chalkboard before beginning lessons. (See video in Lesson 1)

☐ The best chalkboard option is to paint a wall that students can access during lesson time. If that is not possible, a usable substitute would be to either paint the back of the drawing board used in art lessons or purchase a frameless chalkboard.

books.title: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

blocks.text: Have the students spend a few minutes warming up by practicing large arm movements on the chalkboard and drawing a few strokes they have already learned.
Look at the new stroke or letter together. Allow the students to articulate what they notice. How will they achieve the desired look?

Students should practice with a small piece of chalk (on its side) on the chalkboard first so that erasing is easy. Then have students practice the new stroke, letter, or letter combination with pen and paper. They should practice until they have six perfect examples before moving on to the next stroke.
Have the students self-evaluate. They may circle their best examples or reflect on which parts were done well and which ones need more practice.

lessons.title: 20m Lettering: Level 1 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Chalk Lettering 
⍞ Materials: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

➜ VIEW
Watch the tutorial and become familiar with your tools.
∞ Video Link: Lettering: Intro and Tools

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The ABCs of Chalk Lettering" Ch.1 p.3-7

➜ PRACTICE
Prepare your chalkboard. Draw letters using shoulder muscles like in Art Instruction lessons.
lessons.teacherNotes:
• COMMONPLACE BOOK
Write beautiful quotes or mottoes with creative lettering.

Anatomy of Letters
⍞ Materials: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

Watch the tutorial.
∞ Video Link: Lettering: Anatomy of Letters

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Lowdown on Letters" Ch.1 p.8-9

➜ PRACTICE
Draw your name. Use shoulder muscles and incorporate lettering aspects.
lessons.teacherNotes:
• COMMONPLACE BOOK
Write beautiful quotes or mottoes with creative lettering.

Choosing Lettering Styles
⍞ Materials: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

➜ RECAP
Explain the difference between "The Big Three" lettering styles.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Choosing Lettering Styles; What Are Your Words Really Saying?" Ch.2 p.18-19

➜ PRACTICE
Draw words using different lettering styles.

• COMMONPLACE BOOK
Write beautiful quotes or mottoes with creative lettering.

Choosing Lettering Styles (cont.)
⍞ Materials: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

➜ RECAP

➜ PRACTICE

lessons.title: 20m Lettering: Level 1 - Lesson 15
lessons.body:
Finding Inspiration
⍞ Materials: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Finding Inspiration, Sketching Designs" Ch.2 p.20

➜ PRACTICE
Set aside an "inspiration space" in your Commonplace Book to collect inspirational quotes that you would like to use in your chalk designs this term. Spend time today searching for inspiration and adding to that list.
lessons.teacherNotes:
• COMMONPLACE BOOK
Write beautiful quotes or mottoes with creative lettering.

➜ PRACTICE

Composition: Guidelines
⍞ Materials: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

➜ VIEW
Watch the tutorial.
∞ Video Link: Lettering: Composition: Guidelines

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Placing Chalk Guides" Ch.2 p.21

➜ PRACTICE
Set up composition guidelines on the chalkboard for one of your pencil and paper sketches. Remember you are only practicing guidelines at this time. You are not completing projects with lettering.

lessons.title: 20m Lettering: Level 1 - Lesson 20
lessons.body:
Principles of Design (cont.)
⍞ Materials: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

➜ RECAP
Choose one of the three designs and dissect it using the five main principles of design.
∞ Image Link: Dissecting Designs

➜ PRACTICE
Choose one of your pencil and paper sketches and rework the composition, incorporating the principles of design.

lessons.title: 20m Lettering: Level 1 - Lesson 33
lessons.body:
Chalkboard Project 3
⍞ Materials: The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering

➜ PRACTICE
Put together all of the pieces learned this term and plan a chalkboard project. Set up a composition using guidelines to incorporate principles of design. Vary the letter styles, line width, and value. Focus on controlling dust, outlining and drawing dimensional letters. Complete over three weeks.

terms.content: · Show your completed Chalkboard Project 1 to an adult.

· Show your completed Chalkboard Project 2 to an adult.

· The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering: Explain your understanding of the five main principles of design.

· Show your completed Chalkboard Project 3 to an adult.

· The Complete Book of Chalk Lettering: Discuss why "chalk dust can be your friend."
